title: Channel Estimation (pilot-aided wireless channel identification)
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is widefield-fluorescence boilerplate. Physics grounded in Tong-Perreau 1998 blind channel ID; Liu-Giannakis-Liu 2001 survey; Bajwa et al. 2010 CS channel est; Alkhateeb et al. 2014 mmWave; 3GPP TS 38.211 5G NR pilot structure.

E.description: A transmitted OFDM/MIMO waveform passes through a multipath fading channel with impulse response h[tau, nu] in delay-Doppler space; the receiver collects y_k = sum_l h_l * x_{k-l} + n_k and uses known pilot symbols to estimate h.
E.forward_model: y = H(theta) * x + n;  H is Toeplitz (SISO) or block-Toeplitz (MIMO-OFDM)
E.world_state_x: Channel impulse response h in C^{L_ch} (or tensor in C^{Nt x Nr x L_ch}) with L_ch sparse taps
E.observation_y: Received pilot samples y in C^{N_pilot}
physical_parameters_theta: Number of transmit/receive antennas Nt, Nr
Channel length L_ch and number of significant taps k
Doppler spread f_d*T, delay spread tau_rms
SNR, carrier frequency offset (CFO), sampling clock offset

W.uniqueness: when pilot spacing satisfies Nyquist (delta_f <= 1/tau_max, delta_t <= 1/(2 f_d))
W.regime: Well-posed under Nyquist pilot spacing; sub-Nyquist recoverable via sparse CS when channel is sparse in delay-Doppler. Mismatch: CFO, phase noise, non-ideal pilot power allocation.

C.solver_class: LS, MMSE, DFT-based, LMMSE-interpolation, sparse (OMP, LASSO), Bayesian (message-passing), deep (ChannelNet, DeepRx)
C.error_bound: NMSE = ||h_hat - h||^2 / ||h||^2 ~ sigma^2 / (alpha_p * SNR * L_ch); CS recovery ~ k*log(L_ch)/N_pilot
C.complexity: LS: O(N_pilot^3) or FFT O(N*log(N)); MMSE: O(N_pilot^3); OMP: O(k*N_pilot*L_ch)
